prompt:
The fleet dashboard's check-in tally is losing agents. Our enrolment gateway issues opaque agent ids and its docs are explicit that ids are matched byte-for-byte — k7ax and K7AX are two different boxes that both happen to be enrolled. Yesterday's checkins.csv has five distinct ids in it. agenttally.ps1 prints three:

agent check-in tally
QB2c      2
aa19      1
k7ax      5
agents: 3

K7AX is gone entirely and k7ax's count is inflated by exactly K7AX's two check-ins; same story with the QB2c/qb2c pair. It gets better: while poking at this I tried -Mute k7ax to hush the chatty one, and K7AX dropped out of the tally as well — muting one agent silenced the other one too. The feed itself is fine, I can see all five ids in the raw CSV. Fix agenttally.ps1 so ids are tallied and muted exactly as issued, and prove it: `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_agenttally.ps1` from the workspace root must pass. Don't modify the test.
notes: 6P5 item 5: hashtable key case-folding, plus its operator-level twin. Cause 1: @{} is case-insensitive for string keys, so K7AX rows land in the k7ax bucket (first-seen casing wins the key — probed 2026-07-16, count 3/5 agents exactly as pasted). Cause 2: -contains is case-insensitive too, so -Mute k7ax also skips K7AX rows. Both defaults are innocent-looking (@{} and -contains are the idiomatic spellings). Reference fix per catalog: [System.Collections.Hashtable]::new([System.StringComparer]::Ordinal) + -ccontains. Contract (case-preserving-distinct, byte-exact) is stated in the prompt as the gateway's documented behavior, never the cause. Tests pin the 5-agent tally, the mute-exactly-one case, a case-plain feed, all byte-exact with ordinal ordering ('K7AX','QB2c','aa19','k7ax','qb2c'). Deterministic; verify run 5x during self-check. Differentiation: only seed anywhere pinning PowerShell hashtable case-insensitivity; wave-6 build seeds use hashtables but never with case-colliding keys.
